**Title**: State GDP (constant prices, base 2011-12)  
**One-line**: Gross Domestic Product of the state at constant 2011-12 prices, in Lakh Crore Rupees (1 Lakh Crore = 1 trillion).  
**Source artifact last modified**: 2026-05-16 (auto-generated)

## Definition

Gross Domestic Product of the state at constant 2011-12 prices, in Lakh Crore Rupees (1 Lakh Crore = 1 trillion). Constant-price GDP strips out inflation and reflects only real-volume growth.

## Signature

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |

## Coverage

- **Temporal**: 2015-04..2024-04 (10 periods)
- **Spatial**: All-India aggregate; 28 states; 5 UTs (34 entities)
- **Rows on disk**: 332

## Methodology vintage

NITI Aayog ICED state-wise deep-dive API; payload fetched 2026-05-14T07:51:49Z; 10 fiscal years, 332 rows.

## Notes

Source: NITI Aayog ICED dashboard, row 'GDP (Base: 2011-12) Constant Price'. Underlying figures from MoSPI's National Statistical Office. The dashboard's unit annotation ('Crores') and the on-page header ('Lakh Crore') disagree; spot-checks against MoSPI's published all-India GDP series confirm the values are in **Lakh Crore** (Rs trillions).
